tweet: So we wait for the prosecutions to begin and trials of the accused to happen. Citizens will get information from that in drips and drabs. Some but not all will be held to account. People have already taken sides. As with everything political, not many people change their mind.

name: Reid Ribble
tweet: Can congress get to the bottom of it? Sure. But is this likely? No. If congress does it there will be a majority and minority report and they won’t agree with causes or solutions. Nor would it likely result in any legislative fixes. And people will only agree with their side.

name: Reid Ribble
tweet: A complete study is needed. What caused the riot? Who lead them? How many people joined in. What was the extent of the physical damage? How many people were injured? How many died? What weapons were used? Did any politicians give aid and comfort to the insurrectionists?

name: Reid Ribble
tweet: By having a commission of non elected experts do the study and issue a report it is less likely to get politicized by the commissioners. Congress then could, but likely would not, act on the report. More importantly though, citizens would get a broader look at what happened.

name: Reid Ribble
tweet: There are legit reasons to vote against a Jan 6 commission. Outsiders have no legislative authority for example. Congress shirks their own responsibility by passing it off to others. Congressional committees should do the investigation. All good reasons. And also wrong.

tweet: It's not all that hard. Both sides do it. As you watch the campaigns during the next weeks be aware of how and where these tools are being used against you.

name: Reid Ribble
tweet: And step 5 - Victor: Finally, by using this age-old method of manipulation, politicians become a victor at the polls. It’s pretty straight forward and the candidate that excels at it the most will likely experience  victory this November.

name: Reid Ribble
tweet: Step 4 - Voters: By convincing victims that they can vanquish the villain, victims are converted to voters. Voters bring power to politicians. It is the only path to victory. It’s the only way to become the victor.

name: Reid Ribble
tweet: Step 3 - Vanquish: So, politicians seek to convince victims that only they can vanquish the villain. Only they can destroy and defeat the threat against your family and livelihood. When they do victims become voters.

name: Reid Ribble
tweet: Step 2 - Victim: Nobody wants to be villainized because it makes them feel like a victim. Victims lash out. Victims get motivated. Victims feel fear and demand justice. Victims need someone to vanquish the villain.

name: Reid Ribble
tweet: But once it catches on the villain creates fear and possibly even outrage. Most importantly the villain creates a victim.

name: Reid Ribble
tweet: Villain: To win an election you first must create or identify a villain. The villain could be a system like socialism, liberalism, Trumpism. It could be economics like high taxes or debt. It could be an identity group like immigrants, the media or even the police.

name: Reid Ribble
tweet: Well with just over 60 days to the next election I thought I would share with you how candidates manipulate voters and use the technique to win elections. It's a five step process and it starts with creating a villain.
